summarization.a: X-ray diffraction patterns showing reflections from LiMn₂O₄ by 200 lithium cycles, annealed at 600 degrees and unannealed.
summarization.b: X-ray diffraction patterns showing reflections from LiMn₂O₄ by 200 lithium cycles, annealed at 600 degrees and unannealed.
summarization.c: X-ray diffraction patterns showing reflections from LiMn₂O₄ LiMn₂O₄ by 300 lithium cycles, annealed at 600 degrees and unannealed.
summarization.d: X-ray diffraction patterns showing reflections from lithiated phase by 300 lithium cycles, annealed at 600 degrees and unannealed.

d.question: Which crystal phase is present most dominently in this film after annealing?

c.question: Are there any defects in the deposited film?
c.answer: c	The spectra do not show any peaks labeled with an asterix, so there are no defects.

a.question: What two peaks correspond to the same crystal phase?
a.answer: a	The (111) and (222) both correspond to the same crystal phase.

b.question: What could be the reason that peaks related to MnO2 come back after annealing?
b.answer: b	It could be that lithium diffuses to other regions upon annealing leading to more MnO2 crystals.
